heading: From Bali to Cave Country
paragraphs: Growing up landlocked in the Midwest, the closest I ever got to the ocean was late-night television — losing myself in The Undersea World of Jacques Cousteau and falling in love with a world that felt entirely alien. It would take decades, a move to Huntington Beach, California, and a life-threatening illness before I finally did something about it.

In 2016, I became a certified Open Water Diver through PADI. I was hooked from the first breath underwater. Within a year I'd relocated to the United Arab Emirates with one goal: become an instructor. That dream became real in late 2018, while I was also managing a dive center in the UAE.

From there, the ocean took me to Bali. For four years I ran Prana Dive, a boutique dive center in Amed — teaching everyone from first-time Discover Scuba students to advanced technical divers, with sidemount as the thread running through all of it.

On June 1, 2026, I moved back to the United States to take on a new role: Brand and Customer Experience Manager at Deep 6 Expedition Gear. Different seat, same priority — making sure the gear and the experience around it are built to a standard divers can actually trust.

At the same time, I'm working toward full cave certification in Florida cave country. Cave diving tests configuration logic in the least forgiving environment there is, and it's sharpening the same principles I teach in sidemount — the same ones behind the AXIS Method: when alignment is restored, complexity collapses.
